Exit code 1 ..............................F =================================== FAILURES =================================== ______________ test_display_name_copy_pr_checkout_script_shows_pr ______________
def test_display_name_copy_pr_checkout_script_shows_pr() -> None: """copy_pr_checkout_script should show the PR number in the source command.""" row = make_pr_row(5831, "Test Plan") ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS) cmd = next(c for c in get_all_commands() if c.id == "copy_pr_checkout_script") expected = 'source "$(erk pr checkout 5831 --script)"' > assert get_display_name(cmd, ctx) == expected E assert 'source <(erk...831 --script)' == 'source "$(er...31 --script)"' E E - source "$(erk pr checkout 5831 --script)" E ? ^^ - E + source <(erk pr checkout 5831 --script) E ? ^

- source "$(erk pr checkout 5831 --script)" ? ^^ - + source <(erk pr checkout 5831 --script) ? ^ !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! stopping after 1 failures !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! 1 failed, 30 passed in 0.14s

## Summary
| Test | Result | Details | |------|--------|---------| | 1. `pytest tests/commands/pr/test_teleport.py -x` | **PASSED** | 21 passed in 0.80s | | 2. `pytest tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py -x` | **FAILED** | 1 failure: `test_display_name_copy_pr_checkout_script_shows_pr` expects `source "$(erk pr checkout 5831 --script)"` but got `source <(erk pr checkout 5831 --script)` | | 3. `pytest tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/ -x` | **PASSED** | 81 passed in 0.89s | | 4. `pytest tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_*.py -x` | **PASSED** | 33 passed in 0.19s | | 5. `ruff check` (3 files) | **PASSED** | All checks passed |
The only failure is in test_registry.py where the expected bash syntax uses command substitution with subshell `"$(erk pr checkout ...)"` but the actual output uses process substitution `<(erk pr checkout ...)`.
